page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is built on the x86_64 architecture. Memory capacity is 16.0 GB, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations. In single-threaded benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong performance, with gzip compression reaching 34.67 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.51 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 4032.62 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 28.21 GB/sec, reflecting the physical core limit of two.

Memory performance is mixed, showing average bandwidth on large blocks (44.91 GB/sec read) but weaker write performance (22.85 GB/sec) and lower cached small-block read speeds. For application workloads, the D4s_v7 achieves an average of 7.49 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and static web serving throughput of 8.32 GB/sec. Small LLM inference (135M model) runs at average speeds, but performance drops significantly on medium 7B models. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and basic caching layers.
description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores with hyper-threading) and 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Single-core performance is a notable strength, with strong results in single-threaded gzip compression (34.67 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak due to the physical core layout. With average performance in Redis operations (7.49 million operations/sec) and static web serving (8.32 GB/sec), the D4s_v7 is well-suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web servers, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running at 4.2 GHz. It excels in single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: A balanced four-vCPU virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and advanced Intel matrix extensions for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM on Intel Xeon 6973P-C architecture
High 4.2 GHz clock speed with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression tasks
No local storage or bundled GPUs, with support for Premium Storage
Suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and caching
